subject: Q1 packaging order — EuroPack
body:
Marek,

Happy new year. We'd like to place Q1: 65,000 units ESP-CHOC-350, standard spec as per contract. Please confirm availability and send a delivery schedule.

David

subject: RE: Q1 packaging order — EuroPack
body:
David,

Happy new year! Confirmed — 65,000 units ESP-CHOC-350 available for Q1. Production slot is week of January 26, delivery to your site around February 3-5.

I'm copying Agnieszka Kowalska, our production coordinator, who will track the order through manufacturing.

Best,
Marek

subject: Production schedule — Q1 Strauss order
body:
Hi David,

This is Agnieszka from production. I have your Q1 order (65,000 units ESP-CHOC-350) scheduled for the print run January 26-28, packing January 29-30, dispatch February 2.

Please send your delivery address and preferred unloading hours.

Agnieszka

subject: RE: Production schedule — delivery details
body:
Agnieszka,

Delivery address: Strauss Group, Holon facility, Hamivtza 2, Holon. Receiving hours: 07:00-15:00 Sun-Thu. Contact on arrival: Itzik Peretz, +972-54-1234567.

David

subject: Q1 shipment dispatched
body:
David,

Q1 order of 65,000 units (ESP-CHOC-350) dispatched February 2 via truck DHL Freight, AWB PL-202602-3841. ETA at Holon facility February 5.

Agnieszka

subject: Q2 planning + important update on board spec
body:
David,

Hope the Q1 delivery was received well. Looking ahead to Q2 — we'd like to discuss a few things.

First, on Q2 volume: we can support up to 80,000 units if your demand is growing. Let us know.

Second — and this is the main point of this email — our materials team has reviewed the current ESP-CHOC-350 specification (350gsm board) and has recommended an upgrade to 380gsm for structural integrity reasons. We've had two clients report minor compression issues with the 350gsm in hot weather transit, and our engineering team believes 380gsm eliminates this risk entirely. The 380gsm board is slightly heavier but fits the same die line and print spec, no changes needed to your artwork or packaging line.

The price impact is modest — PLN 1.93/unit vs PLN 1.85/unit under the current contract, roughly 4.3% increase.

I've copied Piotr Nowak (technical lead) and Katarzyna Wisniewska (account management) for any follow-up questions on the spec side.

Let me know your thoughts.
Marek

subject: RE: Q2 planning + board spec
body:
Marek,

Q2 volume — let's go with 70,000 units. On the board spec change — I've noted your reasoning. I'll need to run this by our packaging team and procurement manager before we can agree to anything. The spec and the pricing are both contract changes and would need a formal sign-off.

Can you send the technical data sheet for the 380gsm board so our packaging engineers can review?

David

subject: 380gsm technical datasheet
body:
Hi David,

This is Piotr from our technical team. Attached is the datasheet for the 380gsm corrugated board we are proposing (EuroPack spec: EP-COR-380-B). Key improvements over the 350gsm:
- Edge crush test (ECT): 42 N/mm vs 36 N/mm on current spec
- Stacking strength: +18%
- Moisture resistance: marginal improvement

All external dimensions and print registration remain identical — your lines will not need adjustment.

Let me know if you have any technical questions.

Piotr Nowak
Technical Manager, EuroPack Solutions

subject: Q2 production planning — confirming 380gsm
body:
Hi David,

I'm starting to plan the Q2 production run for 70,000 units. Based on Marek's update, I've scheduled this on the 380gsm board line (EP-COR-380-B) with start date April 6.

Please confirm delivery address is the same as Q1 — Holon facility.

Agnieszka

subject: RE: Q2 production planning
body:
Agnieszka, Marek,

Delivery address is the same. On the 380gsm — I haven't formally confirmed the spec change yet. Our packaging team is still reviewing. Please don't assume this is agreed. I'll confirm separately on the spec question.

David

subject: Q2 order confirmation and invoice preview
body:
Dear David,

Thank you for confirming Q2 volume of 70,000 units and delivery address. I've prepared the Q2 pro-forma invoice for your records:

- 70,000 units ESP-CHOC-380 @ PLN 1.93/unit
- Total: PLN 135,100
- Delivery: approx. April 10-12

Please let me know if you need any changes before we finalize.

Best regards,
Katarzyna Wisniewska
Account Manager, EuroPack Solutions

subject: RE: Q2 order — HOLD on spec
body:
Katarzyna,

Please do not process this as ESP-CHOC-380. The spec change to 380gsm has not been agreed. Our contract is for ESP-CHOC-350 at PLN 1.85/unit and any change to that requires a formal written change order signed by both parties.

I am raising this internally. Please hold the Q2 run until we have clarity.

David

subject: RE: Q2 — production hold
body:
David,

Understood, we have put the Q2 run on hold pending your sign-off. Note that if we miss the April 6 production slot, the next available window would be April 20, which pushes delivery to April 25-28.

We would appreciate a decision by March 30 to avoid further delay.

Marek

subject: Q2 — proceed on original spec
body:
Marek,

After internal review, we are not in a position to accept the spec change to 380gsm without a formal change order and pricing agreement. Please proceed with Q2 on the original ESP-CHOC-350 spec at PLN 1.85/unit. I understand this pushes delivery to late April.

We can discuss the 380gsm upgrade for Q3 if a change order can be prepared and agreed in advance.

David
